# The Kindness Flywheel

> An open-source publication exploring the hypothesis that kindness — operationalized as genuine care at every level of an organization — is the most defensible business strategy when AI commoditizes capability.

The Kindness Flywheel explores what happens when we invest in people's flourishing — not just as a value, but as a business strategy. Not kindness instead of operational excellence. Kindness AND operational excellence — with the claim that in a post-convergence world, kindness is what makes operational excellence defensible.

- Founded by: Geoff Scott
- License: CC-BY 4.0
- Source: https://github.com/kindnessflywheel/kindnessflywheel-site

All content is organized through five lenses:

- **Strategy** — Convergence, trust, competitive advantage, the business case for kindness
- **People** — Education, professional development, culture, and behavior
- **Technology** — Product design, implementation, security and compliance, agent development
- **Practice** — Real organizational stories
- **Meta** — How this publication works, content philosophy, AI copyright, editorial process

Key claims (a hypothesis, not a proven theorem):

1. AI is compressing the execution layer of every knowledge profession (40-50% of U.S. GDP)
2. When any competitor can match your capabilities in months, the human layer is the only remaining differentiator
3. Trust — built through genuine care practiced consistently — is the mechanism
4. Kindness and margin are not in tension; kindness is the operating method by which sustainable margin is created
5. The flywheel works when care is genuine at every organizational level; it breaks when any layer is performative

Key data points:

- AI model gap (US-China): 17.5pp → 0.3pp in one year (Stanford AI Index 2025)
- BCG trust multiplier: leadership support raises AI positivity from 15% to 55% (n=10,600)
- 95% of enterprise AI initiatives produce no measurable P&L impact; MIT attributes failures to culture, not technology
- WEF 2025 top 5 employer-valued skills are all human-centered
- Worker cooperatives outperform by 9% on productivity in knowledge work (Young-Hyman 2023)
- 45% U.S. worker burnout; employee engagement at 11-year low (Gallup 2024-2025)

<!-- phaedrus:llms-autogen:start -->

## Posts

- [The Value of the Software Is Zero](https://kindnessflywheel.org/2026/04/01/the-value-of-the-software-is-zero/): When an AI agent can migrate your financial data and generate clean statements in 48 minutes, what was QuickBooks actually selling you? The answer reshapes how we think about every knowledge-work tool.
- [The Race to the Mean](https://kindnessflywheel.org/2026/03/28/the-race-to-the-mean/): AI is compressing the execution layer of every knowledge profession — and that's 40-50% of U.S. GDP. When any competitor can match your capabilities in months, what's left to compete on?

<!-- phaedrus:llms-autogen:end -->
